en: TO THE CHOIRMASTER: WITH STRINGED INSTRUMENTS. A PSALM OF DAVID. Answer me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have given me relief when I was in distress. Be gracious to me and hear my prayer!
zh: 我公義的 神啊 ！ 我呼求的時候，求你答應我。 我在困苦中，你曾使我舒暢。 求你恩待我，聽我的禱告。

en: O men, how long shall my honor be turned into shame? How long will you love vain words and seek after lies? Selah
zh: 尊貴的人啊！你們把我的榮耀變為羞辱，要到幾時呢？ 你們喜愛虛妄，追求虛謊，要到幾時呢？

en: But know that the LORD has set apart the godly for himself; the LORD hears when I call to him.
zh: 你們要知道耶和華已經把虔誠人分別出來，歸他自己； 我向耶和華呼求的時候，他就垂聽。

en: Be angry, and do not sin; ponder in your own hearts on your beds, and be silent. Selah
zh: 你們生氣，卻不可犯罪； 在床上的時候，你們要在心裡思想，並且要安靜。

en: Offer right sacrifices, and put your trust in the LORD.
zh: 你們應當獻公義的祭， 也要投靠耶和華。

en: There are many who say, "Who will show us some good? Lift up the light of your face upon us, O LORD!"
zh: 有許多人說：“誰能指示我們得甚麼好處呢？” 耶和華啊！求你仰起你的臉，光照我們。

en: You have put more joy in my heart than they have when their grain and wine abound.
zh: 你使我心裡喜樂， 勝過人在豐收五穀新酒時的喜樂。

en: In peace I will both lie down and sleep; for you alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.
zh: 我必平平安安躺下睡覺， 因為只有你耶和華能使我安然居住。
